item.title: cmd_status silently omits in-progress items from sprint readiness display
item.intent: cmd_status must show in-progress items clearly so users know a sprint is running and what is being worked on
item.description: cmd_status() calls list_ready_items() which filters by readyForSprint: true. Items currently in-progress have readyForSprint set to false by mark_item_failed and related logic, and sprint.json tracks the active sprint separately. But the status output never shows what is currently running — a user who runs 'aishore status' during a sprint sees the item missing from the ready list with no explanation. The status command should query sprint.json and display the active sprint item ID and title if a sprint is in progress.
steps: After the 'Ready for sprint' section, add a block that reads BACKLOG_DIR/sprint.json
If sprint.json has status \!= 'idle' and item is non-null, print a 'Currently running' section with item ID and title
Verify: running 'aishore status' during a sprint shows 'Currently running: FEAT-001 — My Feature'; running it with no sprint shows nothing for that section
acceptanceCriteria: aishore status shows 'Currently running: <ID> — <title>' when a sprint is active (sprint.json status \!= idle)
aishore status shows no 'Currently running' section when no sprint is active
In-progress display does not interfere with the Ready for sprint section
